reasoning: In 2025, Lewis Hamilton and Charles Leclerc will be teammates at Scuderia Ferrari. Hamilton will be 40 years old, moving to a completely new team environment after over a decade at Mercedes. In contrast, Leclerc is in his prime, holds established relationships within the Maranello team, and possesses phenomenal qualifying pace. History suggests that drivers, even world champions, face an adaptation period when switching teams, and older drivers often struggle against elite younger teammates (e.g., Vettel vs. Leclerc, Schumacher vs. Rosberg). Hamilton’s battles against Russell show he is still incredibly strong, but no longer has a massive margin over top young talent. Thus, Leclerc is the favorite, giving Hamilton roughly a 35-40% chance of outscoring him over the season based on consistency and racecraft.
